skill.name: brainstorming
skill.description: Guide structured brainstorming sessions that turn rough ideas into validated design plans before implementation.
skill.summary: Turn unclear ideas into practical design documents through focused questions, options, and validation.
skill.author: DMJGilbert

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backticks, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance indicators. Review found markdown syntax and planning prose, not executable code. Low operational risk remains because the skill may read context, save a design document, and commit it.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are markdown code fences and inline documentation examples. No executable script, command invocation, or user-controlled command construction is present in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are markdown fence delimiters around templates. I found no shell execution API, no script file, and no instruction to run those fenced blocks.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches Prose
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections occur in headings, markdown examples, or ordinary design-planning text. No hash algorithm, encryption library, credential handling, or cryptographic implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill is a markdown-only brainstorming guide. The surrounding context contains no cryptographic operation or secret-processing behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Terms Used as Planning Guidance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections are benign instructions about asking one question at a time and keeping discussion open to hybrid approaches. No host enumeration, network probing, or data collection workflow appears in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are part of a conversational design workflow. They do not request system details, command output, environment variables, or external transmission.

low_findings.title: Workspace Modification Guidance Requires User Oversight
low_findings.description: The skill instructs assistants to review project context, save a design document, and commit it after the design phase. This is legitimate planning workflow behavior, but it can read project files and alter repository history if followed without user approval.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant instructions are explicit and actionable, but they describe normal documentation and git workflow steps. There is no evidence of malicious intent or covert data access.

content.value_statement: Unclear feature ideas often lead to rushed implementation and rework. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through focused questions, option comparison, and a validated design document.
actual_capabilities: Guides one-question-at-a-time clarification before implementation starts.
Compares two or three solution approaches with trade-offs and a recommendation.
Applies YAGNI thinking to reduce speculative features and unclear scope.
Structures validated decisions into a design document with goals, components, data flow, errors, and tests.
Supports iterative revision when the user changes direction during planning.
limitations: It does not implement code by itself; it prepares the design before coding.
It depends on user answers for accurate requirements and constraints.
It may need access to project files to understand local patterns.
It can suggest saving and committing a design document, which should remain user-approved.

use_cases.title: Define a New Feature
use_cases.description: Turn a vague feature request into a clear design with scope, components, and test strategy.

use_cases.title: Compare Architecture Options
use_cases.description: Explore multiple technical approaches before choosing a practical path for implementation.

use_cases.title: Prepare Implementation Handoff
use_cases.description: Create a concise plan that another teammate or AI coding agent can follow.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify a Rough Idea
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to help me refine this idea before implementation: [describe the idea]. Ask one question at a time.

prompt_templates.title: Explore Solution Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to propose two or three approaches for [feature]. Recommend one and explain the trade-offs.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Scope and Risks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to challenge the scope for [feature]. Identify assumptions, risks, and what can be deferred.

prompt_templates.title: Produce a Design Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to create a validated design document for [feature], including goal, approach, components, data flow, error handling, tests, and open questions.

output_examples.input: I want to add notifications, but I am not sure what kind.
output_examples.output: The skill asks one focused question about the notification goal, then narrows the audience, trigger, delivery method, and success criteria before suggesting options.

output_examples.input: We need a better onboarding flow for a SaaS app.
output_examples.output: The skill compares a checklist flow, guided setup wizard, and progressive prompts, then recommends one based on user friction and implementation cost.

output_examples.input: Prepare a plan for adding export support.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a design document covering the export goal, main components, data flow, error cases, testing plan, and remaining decisions.

best_practices: Bring the current goal, known constraints, and any must-have requirements to the session.
Answer each clarification question directly before asking for the final design.
Review the generated design before allowing file writes or git commits.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill after implementation has already started without revisiting assumptions.
Do not ask for every design decision at once when requirements are still unclear.
Do not treat the first suggested approach as final without comparing trade-offs.

faq.question: Does this skill write code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on requirements, options, and design planning before implementation begins.

faq.question: Can it work with existing projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review project context when allowed, then align the design with existing patterns.

faq.question: Why does it ask one question at a time?
faq.answer: Single questions reduce confusion and help the assistant refine the plan from reliable answers.

faq.question: What does the final output look like?
faq.answer: The final output is a design document with goals, approach, components, data flow, errors, tests, and open questions.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no malicious code. Users should still approve any file writes or commits.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
